title: Iran air defences engage drones over Tehran as US-Iran war enters day 62
contents:
# Iran air defences engage drones over Tehran as US-Iran war enters day 62

## Overview
The Independent live blog and BBC World coverage report that Iranian air defences fired at unidentified drones over Tehran shortly before 22:00 UTC on April 30, 2026, as the US-Iran war passed day 62. Air defence batteries across the Iranian capital activated repeatedly during the evening hours, with Iranian state media confirming engagement of inbound aerial targets without disclosing origin or attribution.

## Key Developments
- Air defence systems opened fire over Tehran during evening hours
- Reports surface alongside Trump's statement that Iran 'wants a deal badly'
- Iranian Revolutionary Guards officials warn of 'long and painful strikes' against US warships and bases if attacks resume
- Trump faces a Friday deadline either to end the war or to extend the operation through congressional authorization
- Oil prices traded above $123 per barrel during the corresponding session, with the war's running cost cited at $25 billion

## Strategic Context
The Tehran engagements followed Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's earlier statement promising 'new management' of the Strait of Hormuz under Iranian control, framed as a vow to eliminate US 'abuses' of the waterway. The pattern of evening defensive engagements coincides with the Trump administration's review of escalation options including additional strikes, special-forces raids to seize uranium, or extension of the existing US naval blockade.

## Tags
iran, tehran, air-defense, drone, us-iran-war, escalation, strait-of-hormuz
summary: Iranian air defences engaged drones over Tehran during evening hours on April 30, 2026, as the US-Iran war passed its 62nd day amid threats from Tehran of 'long and painful strikes' on US bases and warships if attacks resume.
